PCCT Qualification — Gate Findings Report · Version 2 (current)

Generated 2026-07-09 10:09 from gate_results/gate_summary.txt (variance-component wCV, default config), gate_results/variants/, tracker/*.md, paired SNR, and case-summary spreadsheet. Reference: B.1P Delta Validation OQ (730-CVV-040 v0.1) and Original B.1P OQ (4-B1P-033 v2.0). The original analysis is frozen in gate_results_v1_original/.

Executive Summary

Basis: variance-component wCV with scanner term (systematic modality bias removed → the like-for-like analogue of the OQ inter-operator wCV), canonical region. Methodology: tracker/statistical-methodology.md; alternative configurations (legacy / no-scanner-term / OQ-bias criterion) are in gate_results/variants/. N = 25 paired patients, preliminary (target ≥ 30); PT-124 excluded (no overlapping vessel). Latest 2026-07-07 workitem data.
GateRequirementStatus
Gate 1 — Technical & Image QualityDICOM, contrast timing, SNR, kernelPASS
Gate 2 — Workflow IntegrationIngestion, centerline, lumen/wall editingREVIEW — PCCT traces +26% longer than EID on shared vessels (coverage difference > 15%)
Gate 3 — Reproducibility (wCV)95% CI overlap with delta OQPASS (variance-component + scanner-term — see table below)
Gate 4 — Bias & AgreementBA bias vs OQNonCALC Matrix & Total Plaque bias FAIL (both criteria)

Methodology Notes

Vessel-overlap normalization (canonical as of 2026-05-08). For each PCCT/EID pair, summaries are summed only over the (bodySite, vessel-location) intersection — so the same anatomical extent is measured on each scanner. Patients with no overlapping vessel are excluded (PT-124). Most pairs are partial-overlap; PCCT typically traces 1-3 additional distal vessels (PDA, PLB, marginals, diagonals).
Metric per endpoint family. Log-wCV (and log-scale Bland-Altman) for process outputs — Lumen, Wall, Vessel Volume — matching 4-B1P-033 Table 7 and 730-CVV-040 Table 6. Untransformed wCV (and untransformed BA) for plaque volumes (CALC, LRNC, NonCALC Matrix, Total Plaque), matching 4-B1P-033 Table 9 reporting scale.
Wall bias threshold is project-specific. |bias| < 10% of mean was added 2026-05-08 in response to case-review finding (PT-142) of likely true modality bias for Wall at high disease. Not derivable from 4-B1P-033 or 730-CVV-040 (both assess Wall reproducibility by wCV only). Threshold matched to plaque-component bias thresholds since Wall segmentation is dominated by the wall-plaque boundary.

Gate 1 — Technical & Image Quality Prerequisites

1.2 Contrast Timing — paired aortic enhancement

Threshold: peak aortic HU ≥ 250 in ≥ 90% of cases. PCCT 28/28 (100%) PASS. EID 24/25 (96%) PASS. Only PT-124 (242 HU) below threshold.

1.3 Image Noise — paired SNR

SNR = mean_HU / std_HU within a 10 mm aortic ROI at centroid. Threshold: PCCT SNR non-inferior to EID (ratio ≥ 0.85). PCCT mean SNR ~2× EID; lower noise in 24/25 paired cases. PT-142 and PT-158 EID excluded — Aorta.nrrd not generated.




Reviewer commentary: Editable space for Gate 1 notes.








Gate 2 — Workflow Integration


2.2 Coverage → REVIEW: the mean vessel-length difference is +26% — PCCT analysts trace further distal into the same named vessels than EID — exceeding the <15% threshold (proxy for ≥95% coverage). This is a tracing-depth/coverage difference, not a technical failure: the paired analysis is restricted to the vessel-overlap intersection to control it, and the sub-segment pass matches extent directly.





2.4 Lumen & Wall Editing — analyst effort comparison



Per-case analyst effort (1=most effort, 5=least), recorded in PCCT_CCTA_Case_Summaries.xlsx. Paired N=26 patients with numeric scores on both scans. PCCT mean 2.85, CCTA mean 2.65; delta +0.19 (median 0.0). Symmetric distribution → PCCT does not impose meaningfully higher editing effort.




Reviewer commentary: Centerline coverage and editing-effort findings.








Gate 3 — Quantitative Reproducibility


OQ reference vs PCCT result — wCV with 95% CIs


Variance-component estimator with scanner term (systematic modality bias removed), canonical region, N=25. Primary metric: log-wCV for process outputs, untransformed for plaque. Acceptance = 95% CI overlap with the delta OQ. Alternative configurations (legacy / no-scanner-term) in gate_results/variants/.


EndpointMetricOQ delta wCV [95% CI]PCCT wCV [95% CI]CI overlap
Lumen Volume (mm³)log-wCV7.5% [5.9, 9.3]11.23% [8.30, 13.28]overlap ✓
Wall Volume (mm³)log-wCV13.1% [10.2, 16.0]17.92% [14.24, 20.67]overlap ✓
Vessel Volume (mm³)log-wCV8.7% [6.8, 10.6]12.58% [9.92, 14.37]overlap ✓
Vessel Length (mm)untransf-wCV13.88% [10.72, 17.38]21.92% [16.57, 26.45]overlap ✓
CALC Volume (mm³)untransf-wCV25.78% [18.7, 35.75]33.60% [20.73, 46.73]overlap ✓
CALC Volume (mm³)log-wCV4.5% [3.5, 5.4]8.99% [5.71, 11.67]no overlap ✗
LRNC Volume (mm³)untransf-wCV78.8% [48.09, 163.4]98.20% [43.37, 162.72]overlap ✓
LRNC Volume (mm³)log-wCV5.4% [4.1, 6.9]7.14% [3.70, 10.40]overlap ✓
NonCALC Matrix Volume (mm³)untransf-wCV32.61% [24.87, 41.94]40.76% [27.14, 52.28]overlap ✓
NonCALC Matrix Volume (mm³)log-wCV13.6% [10.8, 16.4]17.31% [13.33, 20.33]overlap ✓
Total Plaque Volume (mm³)untransf-wCV27.08% [20.68, 34.74]37.85% [24.33, 50.17]overlap ✓
Total Plaque Volume (mm³)log-wCV13.2% [10.4, 16.0]18.50% [14.07, 22.03]overlap ✓



Reviewer commentary: With the scanner term (bias removed) the process outputs (Lumen/Wall/Vessel) come within the OQ CIs — the residual cross-scanner dispersion is non-inferior to OQ inter-operator variability. Plaque wCVs overlap the (wide) OQ CIs. Without the scanner term the raw cross-scanner wCV is inflated by the systematic modality bias, which is instead assessed in Gate 4. See tracker/statistical-methodology.md.








Gate 4 — Bias & Agreement


OQ reference vs PCCT result — plaque Bland-Altman bias (log scale) with 95% CIs


Reported on both scales. Log(x+1) (matches 730-CVV-040 Table 6; raw plaque volumes are heteroscedastic): acceptance = PCCT bias 95% CI overlaps the OQ Table 6 bias 95% CI (the BA plots below overlay the OQ bias line, OQ bias CI, and OQ LoA band so overlap is visible). Untransformed (non-log): bias in mm³ and as % of mean vs the project-specific threshold (|bias| < 5% lumen / 10% wall & plaque; not OQ-derived).


EndpointOQ bias [95% CI] (log)OQ LoA (log)PCCT log bias [95% CI]log CI overlapPCCT untransf. bias (mm³)% of mean<thr

LRNC Volume (mm³)-0.03 [-0.06, -0.01][-0.21, 0.14]-0.1507 [-0.6961, 0.3931]overlap ✓-4.1817.2% (<10%)fail
NonCALC Matrix Volume (mm³)-0.07 [-0.19, 0.05][-0.82, 0.67]-0.5008 [-0.6522, -0.3584]no overlap ✗-108.8947.0% (<10%)fail
Total Plaque Volume (mm³)-0.05 [-0.19, 0.08][-0.89, 0.78]-0.3433 [-0.4719, -0.2223]no overlap ✗-116.2531.3% (<10%)fail


BIAS & AGREEMENT
======================================================================
N = 25 paired patients
Method: Bland-Altman on raw volumes (PCCT minus EID), per-pair target intersection.
  Process outputs (Lumen, Wall, Vessel): log(x+1) scale.
  Plaque (CALC, LRNC, NonCALC, Total): log(x+1) scale -- matches 730-CVV-040 Table 6
    (raw plaque volumes are heteroscedastic; OQ plaque BA is log-scale).
Bias criterion: pct-threshold [project-specific, NOT OQ-derived] -- |bias| < 5%/10% of mean.

--- Lumen Volume (mm³)  [BA scale: log] ---
  N:                     25
  Log-scale bias:        0.0409
  Log-scale LoA:         [-0.4244, 0.5061]
  Untransformed bias:    39.79 (2.3% of mean)
  Bias threshold:        |bias| < 5% of mean [project-specific, not OQ-derived]
  Bias result:           PASS
  Proportional bias r²:  0.002 [0.000, 0.200]
  Prop. bias result:     PASS (threshold: 95% CI lower bound < 0.1)

--- Wall Volume (mm³)  [BA scale: log] ---
  N:                     25
  Log-scale bias:        -0.2871
  Log-scale LoA:         [-0.8105, 0.2363]
  Untransformed bias:    -136.54 (28.3% of mean)
  Bias threshold:        |bias| < 10% of mean [project-specific, not OQ-derived]
  Bias result:           FAIL
  Proportional bias r²:  0.003 [0.000, 0.158]
  Prop. bias result:     PASS (threshold: 95% CI lower bound < 0.1)

======================================================================



Bland-Altman plots














Per-patient v1→v2 shift — untransformed & log


Each patient's Bland-Altman point for v1 (light blue) and v2 / 2026-07-07 (dark blue); grey arrows show the per-patient shift. Dashed line = v1 mean bias, solid = v2 mean bias. Left panel untransformed (mm³), right panel log(x+1). Canonical region — shows how the systematic bias moved between data vintages.











Reviewer commentary (latest 07-07 data): The Gate 4 bias criterion has two forms — the legacy project-specific |bias|<5%/10% of mean and the OQ-consistent oq-ci-overlap (PCCT log-scale BA bias 95% CI vs 730-CVV-040 Table 6). NonCALC Matrix and Total Plaque bias FAIL under both — a real, statistically distinguishable modality bias (PCCT systematically lower). Wall bias has grown to ~28% across re-processing. LRNC "passes" the CI-overlap test only by low power (its CI is too wide to reject). Full detail in gate_results/variants/ and tracker/statistical-methodology.md §2.1.








Case Reviews




Case Review Notes


Per-patient observations from visual review, captured to inform interpretation of Gate 3/4 results. Distinguishes scanner-modality effects (PCCT vs EID) from acquisition-condition effects (motion, contrast timing, image quality).





PT-133



  • Reviewer:
    • 

    Review date: 2026-05-08
    

  • Findings:
    • 
  - PCCT has motion artifact in the right coronary territory, leading to missed plaque on the right side. EID does not have motion artifact in that area.
  - Bad motion artifact on EID causes overcall of wall and false NonCALC Matrix. PCCT (no artifact) shows the plaque boundary more clearly.

    Interpretation: Discrepancy in Wall and NonCALC Matrix is a product of different imaging time points and raw data, not a PCCT-vs-EID modality difference. The motion artifact happens to fall on opposite vessels in the two acquisitions.
    

    Implication for analysis: Excluding PT-133 from sensitivity analysis is justified because the per-vessel artifact distribution is acquisition-specific, not scanner-specific.
    


    


    PT-142
    


  • Reviewer:
    • 

    Review date: 2026-05-08
    

  • Findings:
    • 
  - Wall is generally more expanded in EID.
  - Outer plaque boundary is much more defined in PCCT.

    Interpretation: Probably a true bias at high disease burden — when wall boundary is ambiguous, even slightly degraded image quality (EID's lower SNR / softer edges) shifts the segmentation outward. PCCT's higher SNR resolves the outer wall edge more reliably.
    

    Implication for analysis: Wall over-call on EID at high disease is likely a real modality-driven effect, not an acquisition artifact. Consistent with the persistent Wall log-wCV FAIL in Gate 3 and NonCALC Matrix proportional-bias FAIL in Gate 4.
    

    

    


    

    Workitem Audit Summary
    

    

    From tracker/workitem-audit.md — audits all 81 workitems in the workflow spreadsheet against the ip3-manager instance and local workitem_summaries/ state.
    

    

    StatusCountMeaning
    

    CURRENT54Local file matches latest instance summary -- used in gate analysis
    

    LOCAL_STALE1Newer summary exists on instance; local copy out of date
    

    MISSING_LOCAL13Instance has summary CSV but no copy in local `workitem_summaries/`
    

    NO_SUMMARY_ON_INSTANCE2Workitem directory exists on instance but no `workitem_summary_*.csv` generated yet
    

    NOT_ON_INSTANCE6Workitem directory absent from instance entirely (deleted / moved / not yet started)
    

    REJECTED / NA5Marked as Reject or NA in the workflow spreadsheet; not eligible for analysis
    

    

    Carolyn Walker's 13 MISSING_LOCAL workitems are intentionally not in the gate-analysis cohort.
